finding: Scope is well-defined: audit only, no code changes unless fixing active leak. 5-layer policy covers all critical gaps without overreaching. Two immediate fixes identified (sanitizedProcessEnv rename, createSidecarEnv filtering) that qualify as active leak fixes.

Each layer is independently deployable and rollbackable. Detection layer is read-only. Prevention (env blocklist) is a new utility replacing a no-op. Scrubbing, guarding, and integrity layers are additive — no existing code requires modification to deploy.

Plan touches 13 packages but only as audit scope. Implementation would be focused: 2 new utility files (secret-detector.ts, env-blocklist.ts), 1 refactored utility (output-sanitizer.ts), 1 new utility (file-guard.ts), plus targeted fixes at ~7 high-risk sites. No package restructuring required.

Critic notes this plan does not cross-reference database_security_doctrine.v1.json or credential isolation artifacts. These should be composed before Layer 5 implementation begins. The Redacted+Effect Config pattern from existing work should feed into ENV_BLOCKLIST design.

Critic recommends placing shared secrets utilities in packages/core (not packages/opencode) to avoid unwanted engine coupling. This aligns with hexagonal architecture — secrets detection is an infrastructure concern, not a domain concern.

Critic recommends first implementation slice be Layer 2 (ENV_BLOCKLIST + sanitizeProcessEnv fix) touching 3 files (opencode-process.ts, cross-spawn-spawner.ts, server.ts) for immediate impact. This is sound — Layer 2 is self-contained, has clear scope, and fixes the most critical gap (no-op sanitization).
